scope: increase language proficiency for study/work/leisure/personal enrichment
develop knowledge, skills, values and attitudes
out_of_scope: generic English learning goals not tied to HKDSE S4-S6 curriculum
primary or junior secondary English curriculum aims
classification_hints: question asks about the overall purpose of the HKDSE English Language curriculum

out_of_scope: cross-curricular links that are not English-specific
implementation details from Chapter 3 or Chapter 4
classification_hints: question asks about the organising structure of the curriculum
common_misconceptions: the three Strands are separate subjects

scope: establish and maintain relationships and routines
converse, discuss, compare, argue, evaluate, justify
out_of_scope: role-play scenarios not tied to English language functions
classification_hints: question involves spoken or written interaction, social routines, requests, offers, opinions in interaction

common_misclassifications.reason: both may involve giving information, but Interpersonal focuses on relationship and exchange purposes

out_of_scope: academic research methods beyond senior secondary
classification_hints: question involves information handling, analysis, presentation, problem solving or language awareness
common_misconceptions: Knowledge Strand only tests reading comprehension

common_misclassifications.reason: both may involve texts; Knowledge focuses on information and ideas rather than imaginative response

scope: give expression to imaginative ideas and personal experience through oral/written/performative means
out_of_scope: full literary criticism at university level
classification_hints: question involves literary response, personal narrative, creative expression or performative language
common_misconceptions: Experience Strand is only for leisure reading

common_misclassifications.reason: both may use texts; Experience focuses on affective and imaginative response

scope: Language Skills and Language Development Strategies
Attitudes Specific to English Language Learning
out_of_scope: Appendix 2 vocabulary-building strategies as teaching guidance
classification_hints: question asks about what students should learn in terms of forms, functions, skills or attitudes

out_of_scope: exhaustive grammar rules beyond senior secondary
classification_hints: question concerns grammar, vocabulary, text type or communicative function
common_misconceptions: language forms are only about grammar

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve textual knowledge; Language Forms focuses on form/function, Text Types focuses on genre conventions

scope: language development strategies for Key Stage 4
out_of_scope: general study skills not specific to English language learning
early Key Stage strategies not listed for S4-S6
classification_hints: question concerns one of the four skills or strategies for developing them
common_misconceptions: skills are tested only in the corresponding paper

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve reading; Language Skills is curriculum objective, AO-Reading is assessment objective

scope: examples of text types for Key Stages 1-4
out_of_scope: text types not listed in Appendix 3
classification_hints: question names or requires a specific text type
common_misconceptions: any real-world document type is examinable

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve textual knowledge; Text Types focuses on genre and purpose

out_of_scope: specific school module names not in the guide
classification_hints: question asks about compulsory/elective modules or enrichment components
common_misconceptions: Elective Parts are optional for all students

scope: core language learning required for all students
classification_hints: question concerns core curriculum content applicable to all candidates

scope: language arts or non-language arts electives as described in the guide
out_of_scope: elective content not listed in the C&A Guide
classification_hints: question references an elective module or extension pathway
common_misconceptions: Elective Parts are assessed in the public examination in the same way as the Compulsory Part

out_of_scope: assessment objectives from other exam systems
SBA marking criteria details not in Chapter 5
classification_hints: question asks what a paper or task is intended to assess
question concerns Paper 1 or reading comprehension demands
common_misconceptions: reading AO only tests literal comprehension

common_misclassifications.reason: both may involve texts; AO-Reading focuses on comprehension and analysis of given texts

classification_hints: question concerns Paper 2 or writing task demands
common_misconceptions: good writing only needs correct grammar

common_misclassifications.reason: both may use texts; AO-Writing focuses on production

classification_hints: question concerns Paper 3 listening component or listening demands

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve oracy; AO-Listening focuses on receptive listening

classification_hints: question concerns Paper 4, SBA oral performance or speaking demands

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve oracy; AO-Speaking focuses on productive speaking

scope: two to four texts over three years
at least one print and one non-print text
out_of_scope: school internal tests not part of SBA
classification_hints: question concerns the reading/viewing programme or oral assessment administered at school
question asks about performance levels, 5*/5** or moderation
